description:
# Compiler2 semantics/tests: make quicksort frontier contract explicit

Goal: the quicksort semantic-closure contract should describe the real recursive activation frontier, not an obsolete one.

Signal:
- `cargo test --lib compiler2::drive_test::compiler2_quicksort_root_closes_with_a_finite_recursive_frontier -- --exact --nocapture` passes
- the test explains why `partition/4` keeps two stable activations while `append/2` collapses only its non-dispatch recursion

Why:
- telemetry shows Compiler2 already converges to two stable `partition/4` activation keys
- `partition/4` dispatch depends on recursive pivot/rest slots, so flattening it to one activation would contradict current keying semantics rather than prove a bug

Change:
- tighten the frontier test to assert the real `partition/4` contract
- keep the assertion focused on semantic meaning, not raw type-id accidents
- close `fz-rh2.8.2.8` if the only issue is the stale test contract
title: Compiler2 semantics/tests: make quicksort frontier contract explicit
